channel.title: Crack A Brew With AJW
channel.itunes:summary: Runner and Coach Andy Jones-Wilkins talks with trail running athletes, coaches and race directors about topical subjects. Each episode features a beverage and will last as long as it typically takes to leisurely enjoy the beverage.
channel.itunes:author: Andy Jones-Wilkins
channel.description: Runner and Coach Andy Jones-Wilkins talks with trail running athletes, coaches and race directors about topical subjects. Each episode features a beverage and will last as long as it typically takes to leisurely enjoy the beverage.

item.title: Anne Flower - Chasing Down Ann Trason, World Records and Western States Dreams
item.itunes:title: Anne Flower - Chasing Down Ann Trason, World Records and Western States Dreams
item.description:
In this episode AJW cracks a brew with 2025 Rookie of the Year Anne Flower.  They discuss her assault on Ann Trason's Leadville Course Record, her world record 50 miler, and her Western States dreams going into the Black Canyon 100K
 
Follow Anne on Instagram @_flowpow_
 
Check out our website!
item.content:encoded: In this episode AJW cracks a brew with 2025 Rookie of the Year Anne Flower. They discuss her assault on Ann Trason's Leadville Course Record, her world record 50 miler, and her Western States dreams going into the Black Canyon 100K Follow Anne on Instagram @_flowpow_ Check out our website!
item.itunes:author: Andy Jones-Wilkins

item.title: Will Murray - Teaching, Running, and Remaining Calm in the Midst of the Chaos
item.itunes:title: Will Murray - Teaching, Running, and Remaining Calm in the Midst of the Chaos
item.description:
In this first episode of Season 13 AJW cracks a brew with Will Murray, Rookie of the Year for 2025 and an all around great guy.  Along the way they talk about the connection between teaching and running and how Will manages to stay so calm in the midst of the chaotic world of high level ultrarunning.
 
Follow Will on Instagram @willmurray.run

Check out our website.
item.content:encoded: In this first episode of Season 13 AJW cracks a brew with Will Murray, Rookie of the Year for 2025 and an all around great guy. Along the way they talk about the connection between teaching and running and how Will manages to stay so calm in the midst of the chaotic world of high level ultrarunning. Follow Will on Instagram @willmurray.run Check out our website.
item.itunes:author: Andy Jones-Wilkins

item.title: AJW Unplugged: 2025 Predictions, Three Wishes for 2026, a Book Review, and a Personal Update.
item.itunes:title: AJW Unplugged: 2025 Predictions, Three Wishes for 2026, a Book Review, and a Personal Update.
item.description:
In this special New Year's Day Episode of Crack a Brew with AJW we review our predictions from 2025, make three wishes for 2026, and launch a new book review feature as well as providing a personal update on training, racing, and all things in between.
 
Follow AJW on instagram @ajoneswilkins
item.content:encoded: In this special New Year's Day Episode of Crack a Brew with AJW we review our predictions from 2025, make three wishes for 2026, and launch a new book review feature as well as providing a personal update on training, racing, and all things in between. Follow AJW on instagram @ajoneswilkins
item.itunes:author: Andy Jones-Wilkins

item.title: Finn Melanson: Special Year End Episode with Ultrarunner of the Year and other tributes
item.itunes:title: Finn Melanson: Special Year End Episode with Ultrarunner of the Year and other tributes
item.description:
In this episode, for the 3rd consecutive year, AJW cracks a brew with Finn Melanson to discuss the year in ultrarunning.  In addition to sharing their picks for these prestigious awards they also comment on rookie of the year, surprise of the year, most inspirational of the year, FKT of the year and best trail running film of the year. 
 
Check out Finn's excellent podcast here
Follow Finn on Instagram @runsingletrack
The Chase
Trail Folk
item.content:encoded: In this episode, for the 3rd consecutive year, AJW cracks a brew with Finn Melanson to discuss the year in ultrarunning. In addition to sharing their picks for these prestigious awards they also comment on rookie of the year, surprise of the year, most inspirational of the year, FKT of the year and best trail running film of the year. Check out Finn's excellent podcast here Follow Finn on Instagram @runsingletrack The Chase Trail Folk
item.itunes:author: Andy jones-wilkins

item.title: Annie Hughes: Running Super Long From a Young Age, Returning from Injury, and Thoughts About Longevity in Ultrarunning
item.itunes:title: Annie Hughes: Running Super Long From a Young Age, Returning from Injury, and Thoughts About Longevity in Ultrarunning
item.description:
In this latest episode in the Youth Movement series AJW cracks a brew with 27 year old trail runner Annie Hughes to discuss her passion for really long distance races, returning from injury, and her hopes and dreams for the year ahead.
 
Follow Annie on instagram @annie.a.hughes
item.content:encoded: In this latest episode in the Youth Movement series AJW cracks a brew with 27 year old trail runner Annie Hughes to discuss her passion for really long distance races, returning from injury, and her hopes and dreams for the year ahead. Follow Annie on instagram @annie.a.hughes
item.itunes:author: Andy Jones-Wilkins

item.title: Caleb Olson: Training and Racing With a Young Family, Western States, and the Million Feet Challenge
item.itunes:title: Caleb Olson: Training and Racing With a Young Family, Western States, and the Million Feet Challenge
item.description:
In this episode AJW cracks a brew with 2025 Western States Champion Caleb Olson to discuss training and racing with a young and growing family, his great story about receiving the coveted Western States Cougar trophy, and his pursuit of climbing 1,000,000 vertical feet in 2025.
 
Follow Caleb on Instagram @calebolson123
item.content:encoded: In this episode AJW cracks a brew with 2025 Western States Champion Caleb Olson to discuss training and racing with a young and growing family, his great story about receiving the coveted Western States Cougar trophy, and his pursuit of climbing 1,000,000 vertical feet in 2025. Follow Caleb on Instagram @calebolson123
item.itunes:author: Andy Jones-Wilkins

item.title: Lila Gaudrault: Running the Rugged Beast Coast Trails, Balancing Running and Work, and Her Thoughts Ahead of Her First Time at the Cocodona 250
item.itunes:title: Lila Gaudrault: Running the Rugged Beast Coast Trails, Balancing Running and Work, and Her Thoughts Ahead of Her First Time at the Cocodona 250
item.description:
In this latest episode of the Youth Movement series AJW cracks a brew with East Coast Crusher Lila Gaudrault to talk about her love of the East Coast Trail Running, how she balances running with a full-time health care job and her thoughts going into her first ever race "out west" The Cocodona 250!
 
Follow Lila on Instagram @lilagaudrault
item.content:encoded: In this latest episode of the Youth Movement series AJW cracks a brew with East Coast Crusher Lila Gaudrault to talk about her love of the East Coast Trail Running, how she balances running with a full-time health care job and her thoughts going into her first ever race "out west" The Cocodona 250! Follow Lila on Instagram @lilagaudrault

item.title: Cody Poskin: Running with Joy, Seeking Adventure, and Racing in China
item.itunes:title: Cody Poskin: Running with Joy, Seeking Adventure, and Racing in China
item.description:
In this episode AJW Cracks a Brew with 23 year old Missouri ultrarunner Cody Poskin to discuss his approach to training and racing, the youth movement in long distance running, and his experience running (and winning!) the Ultra Gobi 400K race in China.
 
Follow Cody on Instagram @cody_poskin
item.content:encoded: In this episode AJW Cracks a Brew with 23 year old Missouri ultrarunner Cody Poskin to discuss his approach to training and racing, the youth movement in long distance running, and his experience running (and winning!) the Ultra Gobi 400K race in China. Follow Cody on Instagram @cody_poskin

item.title: Hans Troyer: Overcoming Rhabdo, Western States and His Upcoming Race at the JFK50
item.itunes:title: Hans Troyer: Overcoming Rhabdo, Western States and His Upcoming Race at the JFK50
item.description:
In this episode AJW cracks a brew with 25 year old Hans Troyer to discuss his experiences with Rhabdomyolisis at the 2024 Black Canyon 100K, hist experience at his first Western States in 2025 and his hopes for the upcoming JFK 50.
 
Follow Hans on Instagram @hanstroyer
Check out the film "The Kid" right here

item.title: Kaylee Frederick: Running ultras as a teenager, finding her groove, and shocking the world at the World 24 hour Championships
item.itunes:title: Kaylee Frederick: Running ultras as a teenager, finding her groove, and shocking the world at the World 24 hour Championships
item.description:
In this first episode of the Youth Movement series AJW cracks a brew with 20 year old Kaylee Frederick of Pittsburgh, PA.  In their wide ranging conversation they discuss running ultras at a young age, silencing the doubters, and surprising the field at the 24 Hour World Championships earlier this year.
 
You can follow Kaylee on Instagram @frederick.kaylee
item.content:encoded: In this first episode of the Youth Movement series AJW cracks a brew with 20 year old Kaylee Frederick of Pittsburgh, PA. In their wide ranging conversation they discuss running ultras at a young age, silencing the doubters, and surprising the field at the 24 Hour World Championships earlier this year. You can follow Kaylee on Instagram @frederick.kaylee

item.title: Andy and Shelly Jones-Wilkins: Javelina, Western States, and Sustaining a Healthy Relationship Through Three Decades of Ultrarunning
item.itunes:title: Andy and Shelly Jones-Wilkins: Javelina, Western States, and Sustaining a Healthy Relationship Through Three Decades of Ultrarunning
item.description:
In this episode AJW hands the microphone to his son Logan to crack a brew with wife and mother Shelly Jones-Wilkins.  In their conversation they discuss their recent experience at the Javelina Jundred, their 10 year run at the Western States 100, and some of the keys to sustaining a long term healthy relationship with ultrarunning as a big part of it.
 
Not surprisingly, Shelly doesn't do social media
You can follow Logan on Instagram @ljoneswilkins
Your can follow AJW on Instagram @ajoneswilkins
item.content:encoded: In this episode AJW hands the microphone to his son Logan to crack a brew with wife and mother Shelly Jones-Wilkins. In their conversation they discuss their recent experience at the Javelina Jundred, their 10 year run at the Western States 100, and some of the keys to sustaining a long term healthy relationship with ultrarunning as a big part of it. Not surprisingly, Shelly doesn't do social media You can follow Logan on Instagram @ljoneswilkins Your can follow AJW on Instagram @ajoneswilkins
item.itunes:author: Andy Jones-Wilkins

item.title: Zach and Nicole Bitter: The Twists and Turns of Sharing a Life of Running
item.itunes:title: Zach and Nicole Bitter: The Twists and Turns of Sharing a Life of Running
item.description:
In this episode AJW cracks a brew with veteran ultrarunners Zach and Nicole Bitter.  In their wide ranging conversation they discuss the trials and tribulations of sharing a life of running and some of their fondest memories of supporting one another in some of the sport's most competitive events.
 
Follow Zach on Instagram @zachbitter
Follow Nicole on Instagram @nkbitter
item.content:encoded: In this episode AJW cracks a brew with veteran ultrarunners Zach and Nicole Bitter. item.title: Leah Yingling and Mike McMonagle: Parenting, Running, and the Changing Media Landscape in Trail Running
item.itunes:title: Leah Yingling and Mike McMonagle: Parenting, Running, and the Changing Media Landscape in Trail Running
item.description:
In this latest episode of the partnership series AJW cracks a brew with Leah Yingling and Mike McMonagle to discuss their new roles as parents to young Monte, how they balance both of their competitive running lives, and their unique roles in the ever changing trail running media landscape.
 
Follow Leah on Instagram @leah_yingling
Follow Mike on Instagram @shitinthewoods

item.title: Abby and Cordis Hall - Western States and the Fine Balance Between Running and Living
item.itunes:title: Abby and Cordis Hall - Western States and the Fine Balance Between Running and Living
item.description:
In this latest episode in the partnership series AJW cracks a brew with 2025 Western States winner Abby Hall and her husband Cordis to talk about their collective experience at Western States and how they successfully balance elite ultramarathon training with their busy lives.
 

Follow Abby @abby.k.hall

Follow Cordis @cordisimo

item.title: AJW Unplugged - Perfomance Enhancing Drugs, Javelina Training, and Hip Replacement Update
item.itunes:title: AJW Unplugged - Perfomance Enhancing Drugs, Javelina Training, and Hip Replacement Update
item.description: In this midseason Unplugged episode AJW laments the most recent positive drug test in trail running, gives an update on the Javelina Jundred and provides a little insight on his ongoing recovery from hip replacement surgery

item.title: Jenn and Jeff Browning: Juggling Family, Running, and Life on the Go
item.itunes:title: Jenn and Jeff Browning: Juggling Family, Running, and Life on the Go
item.description:
In this third episode of the Partnership Series AJW cracks a brew with Jenn and Jeff Browning to talk about managing a busy family and a complex running calendar all while maintaining marital harmony and working full-time.
 
Learn all about Jeff and his various projects on his website https://www.giddyupultra.com/

item.title: Cheryl and Karl Meltzer: Navigating the Peaks and Valleys in Running and Life
item.itunes:title: Cheryl and Karl Meltzer: Navigating the Peaks and Valleys in Running and Life
item.description:
In this second episode of the Partnership Series AJW cracks a brew with Cheryl and Karl Meltzer to discuss the highs and lows of a decades long relationship, some of their most memorable experiences over 30 years on the trails, and what the future holds for them as they enter their next chapter.
 
Check out everything Karl is up to on his website https://karlmeltzer.com/

item.itunes:title: Callie Vinson and Scott Traer - Arizona Ultrarunning's Dynamic Duo
item.description:
In this first episode of his "partnership series" AJW cracks a brew with Callie Vinson and Scott Traer to talk about balancing training and racing schedules, coaching, supporting one another, and celebrating the beauty of the Arizona Trail Running Scene.
 
Follow Callie on Instagram @callievinson
Learn about Scott's coaching here https://www.runfastah.com/

item.title: AJW Unplugged - The Western States 100 2025 Show
item.itunes:title: AJW Unplugged - The Western States 100 2025 Show
item.description:
In this most recent edition of AJW Unplugged, AJW discusses intriguing storylines going into this year's race, makes his predictions for top-5 men and women, and shares what his plans are for Race Day.
 
Follow AJW on Instagram @ajoneswilkins

item.title: Topher Gaylord - Honoring Older Runners at Western States and the Future of the Race
item.itunes:title: Topher Gaylord - Honoring Older Runners at Western States and the Future of the Race
item.description:
In this episode AJW cracks a brew with Topher Gaylord, veteran ultrarunner and President of the Board of Directors of the Western States 100.  In their conversation they discuss the tradition of honoring older runners at Western States, the outlook for the 2025 race, and the long term future of the event.
 
Follow Topher on Instagram @tgaylord
item.content:encoded: In this episode AJW cracks a brew with Topher Gaylord, veteran ultrarunner and President of the Board of Directors of the Western States 100. In their conversation they discuss the tradition of honoring older runners at Western States, the outlook for the 2025 race, and the long term future of the event. Follow Topher on Instagram @tgaylord

item.title: Jan Vleck - From Boston to Olympic Valley, transitioning from road marathons to Western States
item.itunes:title: Jan Vleck - From Boston to Olympic Valley, transitioning from road marathons to Western States
item.description: In this episode AJW cracks a brew with 72 year old Jan Vleck from Olympia, Washington to discuss his transition from roads to trails, his love of the Pacific Northwest Trail scene, and his specific training on the road to the 2025 Western States.

item.title: AJW Unplugged: Reflections on My Cocodona 250 Experience
item.itunes:title: AJW Unplugged: Reflections on My Cocodona 250 Experience
item.description:
In this episode of AJW Unplugged AJW reflects on his recent experience running the Cocodona 250.  Along the way he ruminates about some of the people he met, the lessons he learned, and the ways in which the event will likely alter the way he makes his way in the world.
 
Follow AJW on Instagram @ajoneswilkins
item.content:encoded: In this episode of AJW Unplugged AJW reflects on his recent experience running the Cocodona 250. Follow AJW on Instagram @ajoneswilkins

item.title: Nick Bassett - The Oldest Western States Finisher Attempts to Break His Own Record
item.itunes:title: Nick Bassett - The Oldest Western States Finisher Attempts to Break His Own Record
item.description: In this episode AJW cracks a brew with 80 year old Nick Bassett who last finished Western States at the age of 73 and is attempting to become the first 80 year old to complete the race.  In this wide ranging discussion they discuss training, race execution, and Nick's long history with the event.
item.content:encoded: In this episode AJW cracks a brew with 80 year old Nick Bassett who last finished Western States at the age of 73 and is attempting to become the first 80 year old to complete the race. In this wide ranging discussion they discuss training, race execution, and Nick's long history with the event.
item.itunes:author: Andy Jones-Wilkins

item.title: Ed Willson - Returning to Western States 20 Years Later, Reflections and Revelations
item.itunes:title: Ed Willson - Returning to Western States 20 Years Later, Reflections and Revelations
item.description: In this episode AJW Cracks a brew with 70 year old Oregonian Ed Willson to talk about his training for Western States and what he feels are the "Four Pillars of Success" for older Western States runners.
item.content:encoded: In this episode AJW Cracks a brew with 70 year old Oregonian Ed Willson to talk about his training for Western States and what he feels are the "Four Pillars of Success" for older Western States runners.

item.title: Michael Koppy - Running Western States after 10 years of trying
item.itunes:title: Michael Koppy - Running Western States after 10 years of trying
item.description:
In this episode AJW cracks a brew with 74 year old Michael Koppy, an accomplished ultrarunner from Duluth, Minnesota.  Michael talks about his career running 200+ mile races and his long 10 year journey to the Western States starting line.
 
Consider a contribution to Michael's favorite charity, the Duluth YMCA
item.content:encoded: In this episode AJW cracks a brew with 74 year old Michael Koppy, an accomplished ultrarunner from Duluth, Minnesota. Michael talks about his career running 200+ mile races and his long 10 year journey to the Western States starting line. Consider a contribution to Michael's favorite charity, the Duluth YMCA
item.itunes:author: andy jones-wilkins

item.title: Michel Polletti - From Chamonix Around the World to Western States
item.itunes:title: Michel Polletti - From Chamonix Around the World to Western States
item.description:
In this first episode of Season 10 AJW cracks a brew with French ultrarunner Michel Polletti.  Michel is one of six 70+ year olds in this year's Western States and in this episode he explains why he is undertaking a particularly unorthodox approach to the race in 2025.
 
Check out Michel's results here

item.title: AJW Unplugged - Western States Golden Tickets, "The Chase" Premiere, and Life as a New Grandpa
item.itunes:title: AJW Unplugged - Western States Golden Tickets, "The Chase" Premiere, and Life as a New Grandpa
item.description:
In this latest AJW Unplugged Episode AJW dives into the last two Golden Ticket Races, talks about the incredible new Cocodona 250 film "The Chase", and shares some reflections on how his life has changed since becoming a grandfather.
 
Follow AJW on Instagram @ajoneswilkins

item.title: Andy Blow: The History of Precision Fuel and Hydration and Thoughts on the Future of Endurance Sports Performance
item.itunes:title: Andy Blow: The History of Precision Fuel and Hydration and Thoughts on the Future of Endurance Sports Performance
item.description:
In this episode AJW cracks a brew with Andy Blow, founder and CEO of Precision Fuel and Hydration.  In this episode they discuss the origin story of Precision Fuel and Hydration, recent advances in athlete strategies, and thoughts on the future of endurance sports performance.
 
Learn all about Precision Fuel and Hydration here
Read Hayden Hawks' Western States Case Study here
Read Robbie Britton's Leadville 100 Case Study here

item.title: Shea Aquilano: Finding Trail Running, the Black Canyon 100k and her hopes and dreams for her first Western States
item.itunes:title: Shea Aquilano: Finding Trail Running, the Black Canyon 100k and her hopes and dreams for her first Western States
item.description:
In this episode AJW cracks a brew with up and coming ultrarunner Shea Aquilano to discuss her early career in running, her 5 year history with the Black Canyon 100K and her hopes and dreams for her first run at the Western States 100.
 
Follow Shea on Instagram @shea_aq

item.title: Joe McConaughey: Eight Year Golden Ticket Quest, Cocodona 250 and Finally Going to Western States
item.itunes:title: Joe McConaughey: Eight Year Golden Ticket Quest, Cocodona 250 and Finally Going to Western States
item.description:
In this episode AJW cracks a brew with Joe "Stringbean" McConaughey.  After seven attempts Joe finally got his Western States golden ticket on his eighth try.  In this episode AJW and Joe discuss his journey to Western States, his experiences and advice for Cocodona 250 and his plans for the 2025 Western States.
 
Follow Joe on Instagram @thestring.bean
item.content:encoded: In this episode AJW cracks a brew with Joe "Stringbean" McConaughey. After seven attempts Joe finally got his Western States golden ticket on his eighth try. In this episode AJW and Joe discuss his journey to Western States, his experiences and advice for Cocodona 250 and his plans for the 2025 Western States. Follow Joe on Instagram @thestring.bean

item.title: Zoe Rom and Kylee Van Horn - Unpacking the Myths and Mysteries of Endurance Athletes Diets
item.itunes:title: Zoe Rom and Kylee Van Horn - Unpacking the Myths and Mysteries of Endurance Athletes Diets
item.description:
In this episode AJW cracks a brew with Zoe Rom and Kylee Van Horn, the host of the "Your Diet Sucks" podcast.  In this wide ranging conversation they touch on the myths and mysteries of endurance athletes diets and some of the ways athlets of all types can sift through the noise.
 
Find Zoe's and Kylee's podcast here

item.title: Hayden Hawks - 2024 Ultrarunner of the Year, His Favorite Memories from a Memorable Year, and Plans for 2025
item.itunes:title: Hayden Hawks - 2024 Ultrarunner of the Year, His Favorite Memories from a Memorable Year, and Plans for 2025
item.description:
In this episode AJW cracks a brew with North American Ultrarunner of the Year Hayden Hawks.  They discuss Hayden's excitement at being crowned ultrarunner of the year, some of his favorite memories from 2024, and his hopes for even bigger and better things in 2025.
 
Follow Hayden on instagram @hawks_hayden
Special hank you to Hayden's sponsors HOKA, Precision Fuel and Hydration, COROS, Squirrels Nut Butter, SIDAS, SunGod, and CurraNZ

item.title: Haroldas Subertas - Upbringing in Lithuania, the Cocodona 250, and Western States.
item.itunes:title: Haroldas Subertas - Upbringing in Lithuania, the Cocodona 250, and Western States.
item.description:
In this episode AJW cracks a brew with 2024 Cocodona 250 winner Haroldas Subertas.  In a fun, free-form conversation they discuss Harry's race at Cocodona, how he ended up in the US and what he is doing to prepare for his first Western States in June.
 
Follow Harry on Instagram @harry_subertas

item.title: Darcy Piceu - High Mountain Running, the Good Old Days and Her Hardrock Legacy
item.itunes:title: Darcy Piceu - High Mountain Running, the Good Old Days and Her Hardrock Legacy
item.description:

item.title: Finn Melanson: Special Year End Episode with Ultrarunner of the Year and other tributes.
item.itunes:title: Finn Melanson: Special Year End Episode with Ultrarunner of the Year and other tributes.
item.description:
In this episode AJW cracks a brew with Finn Melanson to discuss the year in ultrarunning.  In addition to sharing their picks for these prestigious awards they also comment on rookie of the year, surprise of the year, most inspirational of the year, and best trail running film of the year.  They wrap up with predictions for 2025.
 
Check out Finn's excellent podcast here
Like a Lead Balloon by Billy Yang 
Alpine Connections produced by Nnormal

Pictured: AJW (in denim vest) and Finn at the Georgia Death Race 2019, the first time they met.

item.title: Jeff Kozak - Writing and running, 100 Mile Memories, and the Quarry Road Aid Station at Western States
item.itunes:title: Jeff Kozak - Writing and running, 100 Mile Memories, and the Quarry Road Aid Station at Western States
item.description:
In this episode AJW cracks a brew with veteran ultrarunner and writer Jeff Kozak.  In their wide ranging discussion they touch on the intersection of writing and running, some of Jeff's fondest 100 mile memories, and the wild and wooly Quarry Road Aid Station at Western States where Jeff has volunteered for the past several years.
 
Read Jeff's Ultrarunning Magazine column here.

In their wide ranging discussion they touch on the intersection of writing and running, some of Jeff's fondest 100 mile memories, and the wild and wooly Quarry Road Aid Station at Western States where Jeff has volunteered for the past several years. Read Jeff's Ultrarunning Magazine column here.

item.title: Eric Clifton: Ultrarunning in the 1980s, the 1996 Western States, and aging gracefully in the sport.
item.itunes:title: Eric Clifton: Ultrarunning in the 1980s, the 1996 Western States, and aging gracefully in the sport.
item.description:
In this episode of the Council of Elders, AJW chats with Eric Clifton about his early days in trail and ultrarunning, brain an east coaster running big West Coast races, and the art and science of aging gracefully in the sport.
 
Eric does not participate in social media but if you want to see something incredible check out his Ultrasignup page here!

item.title: Geri Kilgariff: Old School Arizona desert trail running and the origins of the Javelina Jundred
item.itunes:title: Geri Kilgariff: Old School Arizona desert trail running and the origins of the Javelina Jundred
item.description:
In this episode AJW cracks a brew with Arizona legend Geri Kilgariff.  Back in 2002 Geri and a few friends staged the first ever Javelina Jundred.  Little did they know what it would become.  Sit back, relax and enjoy some great stories from one of the all-time greats in Arizona trail running.
 
Geri stays away from social media but for a glimpse into the old days at Javelina check out this video from the 2007 race.
item.content:encoded: In this episode AJW cracks a brew with Arizona legend Geri Kilgariff. Back in 2002 Geri and a few friends staged the first ever Javelina Jundred. Little did they know what it would become. Sit back, relax and enjoy some great stories from one of the all-time greats in Arizona trail running. Geri stays away from social media but for a glimpse into the old days at Javelina check out this video from the 2007 race.

item.description:
In this episode AJW Cracks a Brew with ultrarunning legend David Horton.  In their wide ranging conversation they discuss David's intense training regimen in his yourh, his experiences at some of the most iconic events in the sport, and his work in recent years as a race director.
 
Check out David's website.

item.title: David Roche - Chasing down the ghost of Matt Carpenter at the Leadville 100
item.itunes:title: David Roche - Chasing down the ghost of Matt Carpenter at the Leadville 100
item.description:
In this special episode AJW cracks a brew with David Roche a few days after his historic course record performance at the Leadville 100.  In a wide ranging discussion they discuss the history of the race, David's training plan, his nutrition strategy and his flawless race day execution.
 
Follow David on instagram @mountainroche
Read Matt Carpenter's report on his 2005 Leadville finish here.

item.description:
In this episode AJW cracks a brew with Don Freeman, a veteran of over 50 ultras including a handful of 200 milers, to discuss the mysterious 200 mile distance, his thoughts on the evolution of the sport, and his "other" career as a pioneer podcaster with Trail Runner Nation.
 
Follow Don on instagram @trailrunnernation and visit the extensive podcast archives of Trail Runner Nation at trailrunnernation.com

item.title: Rachel Entrekin: Fixed Time Events vs Long Trail Races, her Cocodona Experience and thoughts about the future of super long races.
item.itunes:title: Rachel Entrekin: Fixed Time Events vs Long Trail Races, her Cocodona Experience and thoughts about the future of super long races.
item.description:
In this episode AJW cracks a brew with 2024 Cocodona 250 winner Rachel Entrekin.  Along the way they discuss her introduction into the sport, the differences between fixed time events and long trail races, her Cocodona experience and some thoughts about the future of super long runs.

 

You can read Rachel's blog: Another dumb running blog – y'all welcome (wordpress.com)

Support Rachel's GoFundMe: Fundraiser by Rachel Entrekin : Apparently, running is expensive (gofundme.com) 

 

Follow her on instagram at @rachel__entrekin

 

And, learn more about Tepache. TEPACHE: A Modern Mexican Soda made from fresh pineapples! – De La Calle - Tepache)

item.itunes:title: Emily Hawgood - Early life, Ultrarunner of the Year, and Western States
item.description:
In this episode AJW cracks a brew with Emily Hawgood fresh off her 3rd place in the Ultrarunner of the Year voting.  They discuss her life growing up in Zimbabwe, her incredible 2023 racing season, and their mutual love of the Western States 100.
 
Follow Emily on Instagram @emilyhawgood

item.title: Ian Torrence - Longevity, the incredible 1999 season and the JFK 50
item.itunes:title: Ian Torrence - Longevity, the incredible 1999 season and the JFK 50
item.description:
In this episode AJW cracks a brew with veteran ultrarunner Ian Torrence.  They discuss Ian's early introduction to the sport, his groundbreaking 1999 season and his long term relationship with both the Zane Grey 50 Miler and the JFK 50 Miler.
 
Follow Ian on Instagram @itgoesinflag
Read my feature on Ian here

item.title: Finn Melanson: Special Year End Episode with Ultrarunner of the Year and other tributes
item.itunes:title: Finn Melanson: Special Year End Episode with Ultrarunner of the Year and other tributes
item.description:
In this episode AJW cracks a brew with Finn Melanson to discuss the year in ultrarunning.  In addition to sharing their picks for these prestigious awards they also comment on rookie of the year, surprise of the year, mosti inspirational of the year, and best trail running film of the year.  They wrap up with predictions for 2024.
 
Check out Finn's excellent podcast here!
Here is link for "Lucy's Dad" and here is the link to "The Long Way Home"

item.title: Kaci Lickteig: Full-time work, running, and the quest for her 10th Straight Western States finish
item.itunes:title: Kaci Lickteig: Full-time work, running, and the quest for her 10th Straight Western States finish
item.description:
AJW cracks a brew with Western States legend Kaci Lickteig as she begins the build up to her 10th attempt at the Western States 100.  They also discuss her full-time job as a Physical Therapist and why she loves running in Arizona so much.
 
Follow Kaci on Instagram 
 
Check out our Website

item.itunes:title: AJW Unplugged: Here we go! The World Trail Majors
item.description: AJW does a short solo chat about the newly announced World Trail Majors.  Read more about it at aravaiparunning.com

item.title: Jamil Coury: Running History, the Beginning of Aravaipa, and the Legend of the Cocodona 250
item.itunes:title: Jamil Coury: Running History, the Beginning of Aravaipa, and the Legend of the Cocodona 250
item.description:
In this episode AJW cracks a brew with Arizona ultrarunning legend Jamil Coury.  Runner/Entrepreneur/Business Owner, over the past decade Jamil has emerged as one of the most influential figures in the sport.  In this conversation AJW and Jamil discuss the deep history of Arizona Ultrarunning, the origins of Aravaipa Running and the Legend of Jamil's pride and joy, the Cocodona 250.
 
Follow Jamil on Instagram @jamilcoury and learn all about his incredible company at aravaiparunning.com
 
Check out our website
item.content:encoded: In this episode AJW cracks a brew with Arizona ultrarunning legend Jamil Coury. Runner/Entrepreneur/Business Owner, over the past decade Jamil has emerged as one of the most influential figures in the sport. In this conversation AJW and Jamil discuss the deep history of Arizona Ultrarunning, the origins of Aravaipa Running and the Legend of Jamil's pride and joy, the Cocodona 250. Follow Jamil on Instagram @jamilcoury and learn all about his incredible company at aravaiparunning.com Check out our website

item.title: Zach Bitter: Origin Story, Juggling Life with an Ultrarunning Partner, and the Javelina Jundred
item.itunes:title: Zach Bitter: Origin Story, Juggling Life with an Ultrarunning Partner, and the Javelina Jundred
item.description:
In this episode AJW cracks a brew with veteran ultrarunner Zach Bitter to learn more about his background, hear about how he juggles life with an elite level partner, and his reflections on his recently completed Javelina Jundred.
 
Follow Zach on Instagram @zachbitter and check out Zach's website

item.itunes:title: AJW Unplugged: Javelina, Hip Surgery, and the UTMB/Ironman Takeover
item.description:
In this episode AJW goes solo to give everyone an update on his current life situation and to weigh in on the current controversy regarding UTMB/Ironman.
 
Read Gary Robbins detailed account here

item.title: Caleb Bowen: West Virginia Mountain Running and Snagging a Beast Coast Golden Ticket
item.itunes:title: Caleb Bowen: West Virginia Mountain Running and Snagging a Beast Coast Golden Ticket
item.description:
In this episode AJW chats with West Virginia trail runner Caleb Bowen to discuss the West Virginia Trail Running scene, his experience as a college cross country coach at Marshall University, his alma mater, and his experience snagging a Western States Golden Ticket at the Grindstone 100K
 
Follow Caleb on Instagram @coachbowenmuxc

Check out our website!

item.title: Heather Jackson: Triathlon, Cycling, and a Joyful Transition to Trail Running
item.itunes:title: Heather Jackson: Triathlon, Cycling, and a Joyful Transition to Trail Running
item.description:
In this episode AJW sits down with world class triathlete Heather Jackson as she prepares for the Javelina Jundred at the end of October.  They discuss the transition from triathlon to trail running, the differences in communities and cultures, and Heather's strong desire to secure a Golden Ticket at Javelina to earn a trip back to Western States.
 
Learn more about Heather on her website 
Follow Heather on instagram @hjacksonracing
Check out our website!

item.description:

In this final episode of Season 3 AJW sits down with renowned ultrarunning coach Jason Koop to discuss coaching, some of the most influential coaches in their lives, and the current state of the sport through Koop's unique perspective.

item.itunes:title: Amelia Boone: Obstacle Racing, Barkley, and Hardrock Dreams
item.description:

item.itunes:title: Paul Terranova: The Rocky Mountain Slam and Ultrarunning Longevity
item.description:
In this episode AJW sits down with season ultra veteran Paul Terranova to talk about his quest to complete the Rocky Mountain Slam in the summer of 2023 and his keys to ultrarunning longevity.
 
Follow Paul on Instagram @paulterranova and check out his wife Meredith's website https://eatingandlivinghealthy.com

item.title: Leah Yingling: Western States, UTMB, and the Future of Ultrarunning Media
item.itunes:title: Leah Yingling: Western States, UTMB, and the Future of Ultrarunning Media
item.description:
In this wide ranging discussion AJW cracks a brew with Leah Yingling as she prepares for UTMB.  They discuss her last two years at Western States, the difference between east coast trails and west coast trails, UTMB, and the future of ultrarunning media.
 
Follow Leah on Instagram @leah_yingling and her photographer husband Mike @shitinthewoods

item.itunes:title: Peter Mortimer: Adventures and Misadventures at the Barkley, the Spine Race, and Cocodona
item.description:
AJW cracks a brew (and a scotch whiskey) with Arizona ultra runner Peter Mortimer.  In their wide ranging conversation they discuss the Barkley, 200+ mile races and Pete's "One and Done" philosophy.

 

Learn all about Pete on his website https://www.8moremilescoaching.com/

item.title: Maggie Guterl: The Collegiate Loop, Barkley, and What's Next
item.itunes:title: Maggie Guterl: The Collegiate Loop, Barkley, and What's Next
item.description:
In this fun and wide ranging discussion AJW sits down with Maggie Guterl after her 77 hour run on the Collegiate Peaks Loop.  They discuss variety of training, the ongoing debate between east coast and west coast trails, and what's next for Maggatron as she turns 40.
 
Follow Maggie on Instagram @Maggatronruns

item.title: Tyler Green: Facing Up to the Western States Killing Machine
item.itunes:title: Tyler Green: Facing Up to the Western States Killing Machine
item.description:
In this episode AJW sits down with Tyler Green, two time runner-up at Western States, to discuss the connection between education and ultrarunning as well as the wonder and beauty of the Western States experience.
 
Follow Tyler on Instagram @narrowgreenarrow
 
Read all about the Western States Killing Machine here and here.

item.title: Pam Reed: Western States, Badwater, & Hardrock in a Mere 20 Days
item.itunes:title: Pam Reed: Western States, Badwater, & Hardrock in a Mere 20 Days
item.description:
AJW kicks off season three with a lively interview with 62 year-old ultra legend Pam Reed.  Focusing mainly on Pam's "My Three Runs" Project they discuss the joys and challenges of running three of North America's most prestigious ultras in just three weeks.
 
Learn all about Pam on her website

item.title: Hal Koerner: Coming back to Western States and what it all means
item.itunes:title: Hal Koerner: Coming back to Western States and what it all means
item.description:
In this episode AJW cracks a brew with two time Western States champion Hal Koerner to discuss directing an aid station at the iconic race, Hal's long history with the event and his hope to return to run it again in 2024.
 
Follow Hal on Twitter and instagram @halkoerner
Visit Hal's store at roguevalleyrunners.com
Watch JB Benna's iconic film "Unbreakable"

item.itunes:title: Katie Asmuth: Angeles Crest, Western States and Memorial Day Camp
item.description:
In this episode two time top-10 Western States finisher Katie Asmuth joins AJW to reminisce about her first 100 miler at Angeles Crest 100, why she loves the Memorial Weekend Training Camp so much, and her state of mind going into this year's Western States.
 
Follow Katie's adventures on Instagram @kt_asmuth

item.title: Karl Meltzer: East Coast Trails vs West Coast Trails and the Future of Trail Running
item.itunes:title: Karl Meltzer: East Coast Trails vs West Coast Trails and the Future of Trail Running
item.description:
In this episode AJW sits down with Karl "Speedgoat" Meltzer, the winningest 100 mile runner of all time to discuss what's harder, east coast trails or west coast trails and to ruminate on the future of the sport both of them love to much.
 
You can find Karl on instagram and twitter @karlmeltzer and learn all about his event, the Speedgoat Mountain Races

item.title: Dean Karnazas: Running, Writing, and the Western States 100
item.itunes:title: Dean Karnazas: Running, Writing, and the Western States 100
item.description:
In this episode AJW has a wide ranging discussion with veteran ultrarunner Dean Karnazes,  author of "Ultramarathon Man" and "Runner's High."  Their discussion ranges from the intersection of running and writing to Dean's long history with Western States 100.
 
You can find Dean on Twitter and Instagram at @Ultramarathonman or on his web site Ultramarathonman.com
 
If you'd like some help with your running goals, consider hiring Coach Andy.

item.itunes:title: Dale Garland: Hardrock, Education, and the Future of the Sport
item.description:
In this episode AJW cracks a brew with Dale Garland the longtime Race Director of the Hardrock 100.  They discuss the history of the race, the connection between teaching and being a Race Director, and Dale's thoughts on the next ten years in the sport.
 
  • Follow Hardock on instagram and twitter at @hardrock100run
  • Enjoy this video of Bogie Dumitrescu's 2015 Hardrock finish

item.description:
In this episode AJW sits down with 17 year ultra veteran Devon Yanko.  In this wide ranging discussion they talk about longevity and versatility in ultrarunning, her  Golden Ticket winning run at the Javelina Jundred last fall, and her thoughts going into the 2023 Western States 100
 
Follow Devon on instagram and twitter @fastfoodie

item.itunes:title: Jason Green: The Yeti Trail Army, Tom Green, and Inspiration
item.description:
In this episode AJW shares a glass of moonshine with Jason Green the Grand Poo Bah of the Yeti Trail Runners.  They discuss the origin of the Yeti Trail Runners, the inspiration they've both gleaned from legendary runner Tom Green and Jason shares his fondest Western States memory
 
You can find Jason on Instagram and Twitter at @Yetitrailrunners

Arlen can be found on Strava at @arlenglick

item.description:
AJW Cracks a Brew with three time North American Ultrarunner of the Year to discuss the Ultrarunner of the Year Voting, reflect on Ellie's record setting run at the 2012 Western States 100, and talk about how they both remain active in the ultrarunning community beyond their competitive years.
 
Ellie can be found on Instagram @elliejgreenwood  and Twitter @eLLiejG

Anton can be found Instagram @antonkrupicka

item.title: Jim Walmsley with His Plans for 2023, Life in France, and What he Misses Most About Arizona
item.itunes:title: Jim Walmsley with His Plans for 2023, Life in France, and What he Misses Most About Arizona
item.description:
In Episode Two of Season One AJW welcomes Jim Walmsley to Crack a Brew.  AJW and Jim discuss some of the best things about their mutual hometown of Phoenix, Arizona, the intricate details of Jim's transcendent race at the 2016 Western States 100, and the power of Jim's relationship with his wife Jess.
 
Jim can be found on Twitter @walmsleyruns and Instagram @walmsleyruns

item.title: Courtney Dauwalter and the Importance of Relationships, Education, and Going With the Flow
item.itunes:title: Courtney Dauwalter and the Importance of Relationships, Education, and Going With the Flow
item.description:
In this inaugural episode AJW cracks a brew with three time Ultrarunner of the Year Courtney Dauwalter  They discuss the Western States/Hardrock Double, Courtney's background in education, and her relationship with husband and all-star crew member Kevin.

 
Courtney can be found on Twitter @courtdauwalter and Instagram @courneydauwalter
